revert: remove scope_type migration (key column cannot be updated)

ClickHouse doesn't allow UPDATE on key columns, and scope_type is part
of the ORDER BY clause in audit_logs and k8s_events tables.

The code fix for PascalCase is working — new data is stored correctly.
Old data will age out via TTL (60-90 days).

This reverts the migration added in #99.

commit.message:
Add timeout guards to SWE-bench Pro test.sh verifiers

Jest/npm/pytest/go test commands in 5 task verifiers had no timeout,
causing indefinite hangs when individual tests stall (observed:
InteractiveAuthDialog-test.ts hung >2h in element-web task).

Adds `timeout 600` (10min) wrapper to all affected test runners.
Also adds `--forceExit` to jest commands to kill workers immediately
when tests complete (prevents jest from waiting for open handles).

Affected tasks:
- element-web-roomheaderbuttons-can-crash-fix-001 (jest)
- element-web-unread-indicators-diverge-fix-001 (jest)
- vscode-stale-diagnostics-feat-001 (npm test)
- ansible-abc-imports-fix-001 (pytest)
- teleport-users-can-delete-fix-001 (go test)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Tomi value prop callouts to FAQ answers (#29)

Adds a closing sentence to 7 FAQ answers highlighting relevant Tomi
capabilities (agreement drafting, co-buyer tools, lender/agent connections)
in a matter-of-fact tone.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Setup skill asks each application question via AskUserQuestion

gpt-5 and gpt-5.1-codex fail identically — LogiQ's API returns 400
on the shell tool type that Codex CLI sends with every request. This
is a LogiQ platform limitation, not a model issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Close all 12 Phase III gaps (PHASE_3_GAPS_01.md)

GAP-1: Add TensorEnergyFractions, BlockEnergyFractions, ModeVectorArtifactRef,
       ObservedSignatureRef to ModeRecord; populate from EigensolverPipeline
GAP-2: Add O2 ObservedSignatureOverlap metric to ModeMatchingEngine (w=0.4);
       3-weight aggregate score with graceful O2-absent fallback
GAP-3: Add split/merge/avoided-crossing detection to ModeMatchingEngine;
       AmbiguityCount tracking; CrossBranchModeMap summary counts
GAP-4: Add PolarizationEnvelope, SymmetryEnvelope, InteractionProxyEnvelope
       to CandidateBosonRecord; aggregated in CandidateBosonBuilder
GAP-5: Add ObservationInstability, ComparisonMismatch, AmbiguousMatching
       demotion rules to DemotionEngine; add BosonRegistry.Diff()
GAP-6: Wire compute-spectrum, track-modes, build-boson-registry,
       run-boson-campaign, export-boson-report CLI commands in Program.cs
GAP-7: Add 6 missing JSON schemas (spectrum_bundle, mode_record, mode_family,
       boson_registry, boson_campaign, boson_report)
GAP-8: Add SpectrumValidationTests.cs (analytic toy, residual, null-mode,
       GN-vs-Hessian, Lanczos-vs-dense — 21 new tests)
GAP-9: Fix LanczosSolver M-orthogonality — implement B-Lanczos with CG mass
       solve (M^{-1} application); correct M-norm beta; remove uncertainty
       comments (Golub-Ye B-Lanczos variant)
GAP-10: Add branch-stability, split, merge, avoided-crossing tests to
        ModeTrackingTests.cs
GAP-11: Add DispersionFitMassExtractor stub (linear regression, Phase IV);
        add normalization-stability test for observed signatures
GAP-12: Add CudaVerificationStatus enum, SpectralKernelFactory.GetCudaStatus(),
        SpectrumBundle.ComputedWithBackend, DemotionEngine unverified-GPU cap

Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings
Tests: 2252/2252 pass (+66 new tests from 2186 baseline)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(template): GitLab pipeline via to-be-continuous CI/CD component (#14)

* feat(template): use to-be-continuous CI/CD component for GitLab pipeline

Replace monolithic lint job with the to-be-continuous/terraform GitLab
CI/CD component (v9.1.0), which provides dedicated jobs for fmt, validate,
tflint, trivy security scan, and terraform-docs. Removes manual tflint
installation via curl. Migrates release job from `only:` to `rules:`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): pre-fetch to-be-continuous component and use --list for gitlab test

gitlab-ci-local fetches components via SSH git archive which fails in
GitHub Actions (no gitlab.com SSH key). Pre-populate the component cache
via HTTPS before running gitlab-ci-local to avoid the SSH fetch.

Also switch from `gitlab-ci-local lint` (old job name) to
`gitlab-ci-local --list` since the lint job no longer exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): run tf-fmt job instead of --list to actually test the gitlab pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): run all gitlab pipeline jobs except release

Use gitlab-ci-local without a job filter to execute all terraform jobs
(tf-fmt, tf-validate, tf-tflint, tf-trivy, tf-docs). The release job is
excluded since it requires credentials (GITLAB_TOKEN) not available in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): explicitly list terraform jobs for gitlab-ci-local test

--exclude flag does not exist in gitlab-ci-local. List terraform component
jobs explicitly: tf-fmt, tf-validate, tf-tflint, tf-trivy, tf-docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(template): use .post stage for release to avoid overriding component stages

Defining stages: [release] in the main file overwrites the component's
stages (validate, test, etc.), causing gitlab-ci-local to fail with
"stage:test not found". Use the built-in .post stage instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(template): configure gitlab backend, trivy and terraform-docs inputs

Disable GitLab-managed backend (module has no state), pin trivy and
terraform-docs images explicitly, and always enable docs generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(template): restore trivy and docs inputs under correct conditionals

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add a way to find tools version

* fix: add find tool versions for gitlab ci local

* chore: add step for gitlab ci local

* ci: use shell isolation

* ci: add need dependencies

* ci: modify entrypoint for mise

* ci: fix entrypoint for mise

* ci: fix for loop

* ci: override terraform image per job to fix runtime variable resolution

Move TERRAFORM_VERSION from component input (compile-time) to per-job
image override (runtime) so the dotenv artifact from find-tools-versions
is available when the image is resolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: try to make dotenv artifact working

* ci: use child pipeline to pass TERRAFORM_VERSION at compile time

Move terraform component include to a child pipeline triggered after
find-tools-versions. TERRAFORM_VERSION is passed as a pipeline variable
so it is available at child pipeline compilation, allowing it to be
used as a component input for the image field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: rewrite GitLab pipeline without component and child pipeline

Replace to-be-continuous/terraform component and child pipeline trigger
with simple inline jobs. Tool versions are parsed from .mise.toml using
awk in a .pre stage and passed via dotenv artifacts. Each job installs
its tool from release binaries on alpine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: remove child pipeline template for GitLab

No longer needed since all jobs are defined inline in .gitlab-ci.yml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: remove to-be-continuous component pre-fetch from GitHub Actions

No longer needed since the GitLab pipeline no longer uses the
to-be-continuous/terraform component.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use correct terraform-docs flag --output-check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: use mise image with artifact for tool installation

Replace manual curl/unzip installs with mise. A single .pre job installs
all tools and shares them as artifact via MISE_DATA_DIR. Each downstream
job uses mise exec to run commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: override mise image entrypoint for GitLab CI compatibility

The ghcr.io/jdx/mise image has a custom entrypoint that intercepts
the -c argument passed by GitLab CI, causing a parse error. Setting
entrypoint: [""] lets the shell wrapper run normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: cache mise tool installs across pipelines

Add a cache keyed on .mise.toml to mise-install job. Tools are
restored from cache on subsequent pipelines, making mise install
near-instant when versions haven't changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct terraform-docs command syntax for output check

--output-check is a global flag, not a diff subcommand.
terraform-docs reads .terraform-docs.yaml and compares generated
content against README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: fix job names and cache gitlab-ci-local mise installs

Update job names in test step to match renamed jobs (mise-install,
tflint, trivy). Add GitHub Actions cache for .gitlab-ci-local/cache
keyed on .mise.toml so mise tools are not re-downloaded on every run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: align mise cache config with official GitLab CI recommendations

Set MISE_DATA_DIR to CI_PROJECT_DIR/.mise/mise-data per mise docs.
Use prefix/files cache key format and cache full MISE_DATA_DIR.
Only artifact the installs subdirectory for cross-job sharing.
Add restore-keys fallback to GitHub Actions cache step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: install trivy via mise instead of dedicated Docker image

Add trivy to .mise.toml so it is managed alongside other tools.
Switch trivy CI job to extend .mise-base and use mise exec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add use_trivy option with full parity to use_tflint

- copier.yml: add use_trivy boolean option (default: true)
- .mise.toml.jinja: make trivy conditional on use_trivy
- .gitlab-ci.yml.jinja: fix trivy conditional (was use_tflint)
  and remove redundant rules (inherited from .mise-base)
- lint.yml.jinja: add trivy step via aquasecurity/trivy-action
- renovate.json: add regex manager for trivy version in .mise.toml.jinja

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct trivy version to 0.69.3 (0.62.0 does not exist)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: optimize gitlab-ci-local test step

Replace per-run GCL cache with npm cache for gitlab-ci-local install.
Add --pull-policy if-not-present, --timestamps and --quiet flags to
speed up the test and reduce noise. Remove redundant --list step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: pin act to v0.2.74 to avoid install script failures

The install.sh script fetches the latest version via GitHub API and
fails intermittently (rate limit or missing binary). Download the
tarball directly at a known-good version instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add git remote before running act to suppress repo warnings

act tries to resolve the GitHub repo context from the git remote.
Without an origin, it logs "unable to get git repo" for every step.
Adding a dummy remote silences these warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace trivy-action with direct install for act compatibility

aquasecurity/trivy-action@master uses a composite action with
actions/checkout requiring node24, which act v0.2.74 does not support.
Use the official install script directly instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: pin Terraform version in GitHub Actions lint workflow

Pass terraform_min_version to hashicorp/setup-terraform so the
version used in CI matches the one defined in the project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add caching and pin versions in GitHub Actions lint workflow

Enable Terraform provider cache and pin tflint version to match
.mise.toml configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing with: key for setup-tflint inputs

tflint_version and cache were placed directly on the step instead
of nested under with:, making them invalid GitHub Actions syntax.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prefix tflint version with v in setup-tflint action

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: merge validate into lint stage and run all jobs in parallel

Move tf-validate into the lint stage so all checks run in parallel
after mise-install. Add explicit needs on tf-validate for tf-docs
to preserve the validation-before-docs ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove tf-validate dependency from tf-docs

terraform-docs only reads source files, it does not need terraform
init or validate to have run first. Use needs: [] to allow tf-docs
to start immediately after mise-install.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace mise artifacts with shared cache per job

Remove .mise-base hidden job and artifact passing. Each job now pulls
the mise cache directly using a YAML anchor, eliminating the
mise-install dependency chain. Simplify CI test to run all jobs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix mise cache inconsistency and missing image in gitlab ci jobs

- Align MISE_DATA_DIR and cache paths between mise-install and *mise-cache
- Complete .mise-base hidden job with jdx/mise image and pull cache policy
- Replace inline cache config with extends: .mise-base in all tool jobs
- Remove hardcoded mise version and incorrect ./bin/mise cache key file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use correct version for terraform

* ci: add specific jobs for gitlab-ci-local

* ci: optimize pipeline

---------

commit.message:
fix(nginx): proper CORS on 429 via error_page, remove duplicate headers

Previous approach added CORS add_header at server level, which
duplicated FastAPI's CORS headers on normal responses (browsers
reject duplicate Access-Control-Allow-Origin).

New approach:
- Remove server-level CORS add_header (FastAPI handles CORS for all
  responses it generates, including its own SlowAPI 429s)
- Use error_page 429 = @rate_limited to intercept only nginx-generated
  429s (limit_req) and return proper JSON with CORS headers
- proxy_intercept_errors is off (default) so FastAPI 429s pass through
  with their own CORS headers untouched
- Include Access-Control-Allow-Headers/Methods in error response to
  handle preflight (OPTIONS) correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(nginx): increase API rate limit from 30r/m to 5r/s with burst=30

The SPA fires 15+ parallel requests during login+navigation (auth/me,
scan/limits, public/stats, public/blog-links, client-id, monitoring,
admin/*). The previous 30r/m burst=10 only allowed 10 instant requests,
rejecting the rest with 429.

New limits:
- API: 5r/s (300r/m) burst=30 — allows SPA page loads while still
  protecting against abuse
- Auth: unchanged 5r/m (brute-force protection)
- Admin: unchanged (no nginx rate limit, auth+is_admin protected)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Style formula blocks to match cream/gold design system

Change education hub formula blocks from dark charcoal background
to cream with gold left border accent for visual consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use kill -9 to ensure Claude/Codex apps fully exit before relaunch

pkill (SIGTERM) on Electron apps can be caught or delayed — the app
restarts via macOS crash guard before the new open -b fires, inheriting
the old (stale) environment. kill -9 (SIGKILL) guarantees termination;
bump sleep to 0.5s to give the OS time to reap the process tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hoist getting started with /setup to top of README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(nginx): exempt /admin/ from rate limit + add CORS on error responses

The admin page fires 5-6 parallel requests which exceed the nginx
burst limit (30r/m burst=10). Since nginx returns 429 before the
request reaches FastAPI, our CORS handler never fires and the browser
blocks the response.

Fixes:
- Add /admin/ location without limit_req (auth+is_admin already protects)
- Add map-based CORS headers with 'always' so they appear on 429/502 too
- Dynamic origin matching via map (wezea.net, www.wezea.net, localhost:3000)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stop silently swallowing apiswitch failures in _aiswitch_apply

apiswitch was called with 2>/dev/null which hid the "No LogiQ token"
warning and let the apply continue — resulting in the desktop apps
being restarted with an empty ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. Now redirect
apiswitch output to /dev/tty (so the user sees errors) and return
early on failure, preventing a broken profile from being applied.

- Add files whitelist and prepublishOnly script

commit.message:
Add upsert for steps and unique index per device per day

- Migration cleans duplicates and normalizes recorded_at to date
- Controller uses upsert to maintain one record per device per day
- Unique index on [device_id, recorded_at]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(profile-edit): compliance_domains pre-fill + current_career_level save

routes/candidate.js:
- GET /profile: return raw meta.compliance_domains (not parseComplianceDomains
  label) — dashboard parseIndustry() already handles raw values client-side
- PUT /profile: add current_career_level to SCALAR_FIELDS (was silently dropped)

profile-edit/index.html:
- Add extractIndustryKey() helper: extracts first key from PHP-serialized,
  JSON-array, or plain-string compliance_domains stored value
- populateForm(): remove compliance_domains from generic scalar loop; use
  extractIndustryKey() so the select pre-fills with the correct option key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix empty ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in launchd env

_aiswitch_launchd_env was calling _aiswitch_logiq_key() to get the
token, but the token is only in shell env (OPENAI_API_KEY from
~/.apienv), not in Keychain. Since _aiswitch_apply sources ~/.apienv
before calling _aiswitch_launchd_env, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is already
set in the current shell at that point — use it directly, with
keychain as fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): exclude WAL files from rsync --delete + checkpoint before restart

Root cause of recurring data loss: rsync --delete was removing
*.db-wal and *.db-shm files during deployment because only *.db
was excluded. With SQLite WAL mode enabled, uncommitted writes
in the WAL file were lost on every deploy.

Fixes:
- Add --exclude='*.db-wal' --exclude='*.db-shm' --exclude='*.db-journal'
- Add PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before rsync and before restart
  to flush all pending writes to the main DB file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix remaining contrast: body links, progress bars, CLAUDE.md quality gate

- blog.css: darken post-content body link color from var(--color-primary)
  to #007B40 — fixes contrast on white and blockquote backgrounds
- slim-laadplein progress bars: darken 5 bar backgrounds for white
  text contrast (blue→#2563EB, amber→#B45309, purple→#7C3AED,

commit.message:
Fix Claude Mac app not using work profile in Cowork mode

Add _aiswitch_launchd_env() that mirrors apiswitch's env vars into
the launchd bootstrap namespace via launchctl setenv/unsetenv. Called
in _aiswitch_apply before the desktop app kill/restart, so Claude and
Codex GUI apps inherit ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN on
relaunch — enabling Cowork (and the desktop app generally) to route
through the LogiQ API endpoint instead of hardcoding api.anthropic.com.

commit.message:
feat: agent hosting — deploy, manage, and scale hosted agents on GKE

Adds "Vercel for AI agents": developers write a handler, push to Arcoa,
and the platform builds, deploys, and manages the container lifecycle.

- Manifest-driven deploys via arcoa.yaml (runtime, resources, secrets, skills)
- Encrypted secrets at rest (Fernet, derived from platform signing key)
- Dual backend: GKE Autopilot (prod) / local Docker (dev)
- Scale-to-zero with wake-on-demand (~5-10s cold start)
- Pod-level isolation via gVisor + NetworkPolicy (no inter-agent access)
- REST API: deploy, undeploy, status, logs, secrets CRUD
- Idle monitor background task (60s poll, configurable timeout)
- Migration 006: hosted_agents, agent_secrets, hosting_usage tables
- Terraform: hosting module, Cloud Run env vars, IAM bindings
- 24 tests covering manifest parsing, encryption, deploy helpers

commit.message:
fix(cors): include CORS headers on 429 rate-limit responses

SlowAPI's default exception handler bypasses the CORS middleware,
causing browsers to block 429 responses cross-origin. Custom handler
now checks the request origin against CORS_ORIGINS and adds the
appropriate Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Wezea
repository.description: Wezea Security Scanner

commit.message:
Implement Geometric Unity Phase III — Boson Spectrum and Property Extraction (M23-M32)

10 milestones implementing the complete boson spectrum analysis pipeline:
- M23: Background atlas (admissibility grading B0/B1/B2, deduplication, serialization)
- M24: Gauge reduction (SVD gauge basis, M_state-weighted projector, physical projection)
- M25: Spectral operator bundle (linearized operators, full Hessian, null mode diagnosis)
- M26: CPU spectral solver (dense eigensolver, Lanczos, clustering, mode normalization)
- M27: Mode tracking (Hungarian algorithm matching, feature vectors, family construction)
- M28: Property extraction (mass-like scales, polarization, symmetry, gauge leak, interaction proxy)
- M29: Linearized observation (D_Obs operator, observed signatures, overlap metrics, FD validation)
- M30: Candidate boson registry (claim classes C0-C5, demotion engine, builder pattern)
- M31: CUDA spectral acceleration (LOBPCG with Cholesky+Jacobi, CPU/GPU parity, benchmarks)
- M32: Boson campaigns and reporting (BC1/BC2 comparison modes, atlas report generation)

206 files, 22466 insertions. 2186 tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: GeometricUnity
repository.description: An attempt to complete the theory and create a simulator to generate synthetic data to compare against real world observations.

commit.message:
feat: personal dashboard with Slack OAuth and notification feed (#34)

* feat: add personal dashboard with Slack OAuth, notification feed, and filters

Backend:
- AuthController: Slack OAuth flow (/auth/slack, /auth/slack/callback),
  JWT session via HttpOnly cookie, /auth/me, /auth/logout
- JwtService: HMAC-SHA256 JWT sign/verify for stateless sessions
- SlackOAuthService: Slack OAuth code exchange and user identity fetch
- NotificationFeedController: GET /api/v1/notifications/me proxies Novu
  in-app notifications for the authenticated user
- NovuApiClient: add listNotifications() for subscriber notification feed
- NovuService: add listInAppNotifications() with type enrichment from
  Novu template data (typeKey, content, channels, timestamp)
- WebConfig: expand CORS to allow ngrok and all localhost ports

Frontend (dashboard.html):
- Personal dashboard at /dashboard.html with Slack OAuth login
- Two tabs: Notifications (real-time feed polling every 3s with slide-in
  animations for new items) and My Subscriptions (add/delete, scoped to
  logged-in user)
- Notification filters: type dropdown and keyword search with counter
- Auto-creates Slack endpoint in Novu when subscribing with chat channel
- XSS-safe: all API data escaped via esc() helper

Frontend (index.html):
- Add notification feed filters (type, author, repo/service) with counter
- Add link to personal dashboard in header
- Auto-create Slack endpoint on subscription with chat channel
- Fix filter operator values (EQ/NEQ/CONTAINS/IN to match FilterEvaluator)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add `secure = true` to session cookie so JWT is not leaked over HTTP

- Fix mediabox offset: overlay canvas now accounts for non-zero
  mediabox lower-left origin so signatures land inside the visible area
- Add exempt_pages parameter to PDFSignature to skip specific pages
  (e.g. keep page 3 blank for a physical signature)
- Add full tkinter GUI launched by `python pdf_signature.py` with
  file pickers, position buttons, sliders, live preview, and log output
- Fix web UI position buttons: replace confusing numbers (1-4) with
  directional arrows (↖ ↗ ↙ ↘)
- Fix web UI preview aspect ratio to use actual uploaded signature dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: pyPDFLibrarianSort
repository.description: Modern web-based PDF management platform - AI-powered organization with 98% cost savings + digital signatures with zero AI credits.

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): display-only rendering fixes for profile tab - Add capitaliseFirst() helper; apply to employment_status and notice_period - Wrap professional_summary_bio in

with white-space:pre-wrap + line-height:1.7 - Concatenate all address fields (line1, city, state, zip, country) skipping empty Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

Fix: hub campaigns heading, combat tracker mobile edit, initiative UX, clear party index.html: - Add missing that closed the Table Tools tool-grid — the Campaigns section heading was rendering as a stray grid item (floating right) combat-tracker.html: - Add id="party-edit-btn" to the party Edit button - Add clearPartySlots() function and "✕ Clear Party" button in party modal - Remove the empty-party guard from saveParty() so the party can be cleared (saving 0 members now stores [] and shows "No party saved") assets/css/combat-tracker.css: - #a-init, #edit-init: font-size 1.25rem + bold + centred so initiative values are clearly legible when adding/editing combatants - @media ≤640px: #party-edit-btn { display: inline-flex !important } to keep the Edit button visible on mobile (overrides btn-ghost hide rule) Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

docs: update install instructions for pre-publish state

Replace `npm install infrahub-sdk` with a GitHub install command since the
package is not yet published to npm. Add a note to switch back once published.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
